message:
Add runtime-configurable compaction threshold

Add methods to configure and query the fragmentation threshold used by
should_compact() for deciding when compaction is worthwhile.

New methods:
- set_compact_threshold(ratio: f32) -> &mut Self
  Sets the fragmentation ratio threshold, clamped to [0.0, 1.0]
  Returns &mut Self for method chaining
- compact_threshold() -> f32
  Returns the current threshold value

Behavior changes:
- should_compact() now uses self.compact_threshold instead of constant
- Allows tuning compaction sensitivity per-pool instance
- Lower threshold = more aggressive compaction (e.g., 0.3 = 30%)
- Higher threshold = less frequent compaction (e.g., 0.7 = 70%)

Test coverage:
- test_set_compact_threshold: Verifies setter/getter work
- test_compact_threshold_clamping: Verifies [0.0, 1.0] clamping
- test_should_compact_respects_custom_threshold: Verifies integration
- test_compact_threshold_builder_pattern: Verifies method chaining

Use case: Applications with different memory/CPU tradeoffs can tune
compaction behavior without modifying the global constant.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
